sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP, MOUSE(row=3, col=37).
You are still on the same level.
The game is over.
Current state: step 102, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'SPACE': 'With purple id=12 and 3 objects: splits other object (VERIFIED step 35). With purple id=12 and 4 objects at [21,24]: swaps colors of id=13/id=14 (VERIFIED step 71). With white id=12 and 4 objects at [21,24]: merges id=13+id=14 into solid white (VERIFIED step 73). With white id=12 and 4 objects at [32,17]: converts id=13 purple→white(85px), id=14 white→purple(15px) (VERIFIED step 78). With purple id=12 and 4 objects at [32,17]: converts id=13 white→purple(65px), id=14 purple→white(35px) (VERIFIED step 80). Repeated SPACE with same color: no change (VERIFIED step 81).', 'MOUSE on indicator': 'Paints id=12 (white at col~37, purple at col~45) (VERIFIED)', 'ARROW keys': 'UP moves frame to [21,24], DOWN moves frame to [32,17] (VERIFIED). LEFT/RIGHT only affect timer bar, not frame position (VERIFIED step 86-87).'}
- Object taxonomy: {'id_0': 'black background (top-left region, contains left panel)', 'id_5': 'white rectangle in left panel (50px, rows 3-7)', 'id_9': 'purple rectangle in left panel (50px, rows 8-12)', 'id_11': 'gray frame (moves with UP/DOWN arrows)', 'id_12': 'diamond/block-part inside frame (paintable with MOUSE)', 'id_13_14': 'block pieces (split/merge target, diagonal split)'}
- HUD map: {'top_bar': 'color indicators (white id=6 at col~36, purple id=7 at col~42)', 'bottom_bar': 'timer/step counter (id=15, shrinks each action)', 'left_panel': 'target pattern: white(50px) above purple(50px), horizontal split'}
- Win pattern: Match left panel: white rectangle above purple rectangle (horizontal split). Block currently has diagonal split.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 101 actions; a strong score needs about 55 or fewer. You are 1.8x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 101 actions returned the board to a state already seen 101 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 101 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

[LEDGER COMPACTION]
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'SPACE': 'With purple id=12 and 3 objects: splits solid block into diagonal purple(65px)+white(35px) (VERIFIED step 35). With purple id=12 and 4 objects at [21,24]: swaps colors of id=13/id=14 (VERIFIED step 71). With white id=12 and 4 objects at [21,24]: merges id=13+id=14 into solid white(100px) (VERIFIED step 73). With white id=12 and 4 objects at [32,17]: converts to white(85px)+purple(15px) diagonal (VERIFIED step 78). With purple id=12 and 4 objects at [32,17]: converts to purple(65px)+white(35px) diagonal (VERIFIED step 80). Repeated SPACE same color: no change (VERIFIED step 81).', 'MOUSE on indicator': 'Paints id=12 color: white at col~37, purple at col~43 (VERIFIED)', 'ARROW keys': 'UP moves frame to [21,24], DOWN moves frame to [32,17] (VERIFIED). LEFT/RIGHT only affect timer bar, not frame position (VERIFIED step 86-87).'}
- Object taxonomy: {'id_5_9': 'Left panel target pattern: white(50px) rows 3-7 above purple(50px) rows 8-12, horizontal split', 'id_11': 'Gray frame (30-43px), moves with UP/DOWN', 'id_12': 'Diamond/block inside frame (84px), paintable with MOUSE', 'id_13_14': 'Block pieces (100px total), diagonal split, split/merge target'}
- HUD map: {'top_bar': 'Color indicators: white id=6 at col~36, purple id=7 at col~42', 'bottom_bar': 'Timer/step counter (charcoal), shrinks each action, game over when depleted', 'left_panel': 'Target pattern: white(50px) above purple(50px), horizontal split'}
- Win pattern: Match left panel: horizontal white-on-top, purple-on-bottom split, 50/50. Block starts with diagonal split.
- Level log: {'level_1': 'FAILED - timer ran out at 101 actions. Never achieved horizontal split. Only diagonal splits and solid merge observed.'}
- Cross-level notes: Timer is critical - must solve in ~55 actions. SPACE at different frame positions produces different white/purple ratios. White SPACE at [21,24] gives 90/10, at [32,17] gives 85/15. Purple SPACE always gives 65/35. No horizontal split achieved yet - mechanic unknown.
Current level:
- World model: Frame has two positions: [21,24] (UP) and [32,17] (DOWN). Block is 10x10 at [34,27]. SPACE toggles between diagonal split states based on id=12 color and frame position. Timer depletes with each action.
- Goal model: Transform diagonal-split block into horizontal white-on-top purple-on-bottom split (50/50) matching left panel.
- Recent findings: ['Timer runs out after ~100 actions', 'SPACE at [21,24] with white merges to solid white(100px)', 'SPACE at [21,24] with purple swaps colors', 'SPACE at [32,17] with white gives 85/15, with purple gives 65/35', 'Frame shrinks to 30px at [32,17] position', 'Never achieved horizontal split in 101 attempts']
- Open questions: ['How to produce horizontal split? Is there an untried mechanic?', 'Can MOUSE click on individual block cells to paint them?', 'Does the order of SPACE operations matter (cumulative effect)?', 'Is there a third frame position or hidden state?']
- Plan: Next attempt: try MOUSE on individual block cells to paint them directly. If that fails, try SPACE sequences that alternate frame positions to see if cumulative effects produce horizontal split.
- Strategy log: ['Exhaustive SPACE color/position toggle testing: only produces diagonal splits or solid merge, never horizontal (steps 35-101)', 'MOUSE on block cells and left panel: no effect observed']
- Failed probes: ['SPACE with purple id=12 and 4 objects at [21,24]: no effect (step 62)', 'SPACE with purple id=12 and 4 objects at [32,17]: no effect (step 65)', 'SPACE with white id=12 on stable block: no change (step 68)', 'Alternating SPACE colors at [32,17]: cycles two fixed diagonal states (steps 83-85)', 'LEFT at [32,17]: no frame movement (step 86)', 'RIGHT at [32,17]: no frame movement (step 87)', 'MOUSE on block cells: no effect', 'MOUSE on left panel: no effect', 'Repeated SPACE same color: no change (step 81)', 'All SPACE combos at both positions for 101 actions: never produced horizontal split']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.

The game is over. The image shows the block with purple on top and white on bottom - a horizontal split! But the segmentation says id=13 is white, 100px (solid white). This is confusing.
Wait, looking at the image more carefully:
- The block shows purple on top, white on bottom
- This is a horizontal split, but with colors REVERSED from the target (target is white on top, purple on bottom)
So the game over state shows the block as a horizontal split with wrong colors. This suggests:
1. The horizontal split IS achievable (or happens at game over)
2. The colors need to be swapped
But the segmentation says id=13 is white, 100px. Let me re-check...
Actually, looking at the image again, I see:
- Left panel: white on top, purple on bottom (target)
- Block: purple on top, white on bottom (reversed)
Wait, but the segmentation says the block is solid white (100px). Maybe the image is showing a different state than the segmentation?

But the segmentation says:
- id=13: W (white), 100px, boundary [[34, 27], [34, 36], [43, 36], [43, 27]]
This is a 10x10 square, all white. But the image shows a horizontal split.
I think there might be a discrepancy between the image and the segmentation. Let me trust the segmentation for now.

- Bottom half: white (5 rows)
This is a horizontal split, 50/50. The colors are reversed from the target.
But the segmentation says id=13 is white, 100px. If the block were split, there would be two separate nodes (one purple, one white).
I think the issue is that the image might be from a different frame than the current segmentation. Or the game over state changed the block.
Let me check the history to see what happened. The last actions were UP, MOUSE(row=3, col=37). But the game was already over, so these actions might not have executed.
Actually, looking at the user's message: "The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence. Executed actions: UP, MOUSE(row=3, col=37)."
So the actions DID execute. But the game was already over, so they might have been no-ops.
Let me check the current frame segmentation again. The block is solid white (100px). But the image shows a horizontal split.
I'm confused. Let me just accept that the game is over and move on. The key finding is:
1. The game ended (game_over=True)
2. The block is solid white (100px) according to segmentation

For the next level, I need to solve it faster. The key question is: how to produce a horizontal split?

But I already tried MOUSE on block cells and it didn't work. Unless I need to try specific cells?
Or maybe the solution is:
1. The block needs to be in a specific state (solid white or diagonal split)
2. Then, a specific sequence of SPACE operations produces the horizontal split
Let me think about this differently. The SPACE operation seems to:
- With white id=12 at [21,24]: merge to solid white
- With purple id=12 at [21,24]: swap colors
- With white id=12 at [32,17]: white(85)+purple(15)
- With purple id=12 at [32,17]: purple(65)+white(35)

1. SPACE white at [21,24] → solid white
2. SPACE purple at [21,24] → diagonal split (purple+white)
3. SPACE white at [32,17] → white(85)+purple(15)
4. SPACE purple at [32,17] → purple(65)+white(35)
5. SPACE white at [21,24] → solid white
6. SPACE purple at [21,24] → diagonal split
7. SPACE purple at [32,17] → ???
But this is just cycling through states. I don't think it will produce a horizontal split.
Actually, let me reconsider the image. The block shows purple on top, white on bottom. This is a horizontal split. But the colors are reversed from the target.
What if the game over state transforms the block to a horizontal split? And the game fails because the colors are wrong?
If that's the case, maybe the solution is to:
1. Get the block to the right color ratio (50/50)
2. Then, the game over transformation produces the correct horizontal split
But I've never seen a 50/50 split. The splits are always 65/35 or 85/15 or 90/10.
Hmm, what if I need to:
1. Merge to solid white
2. Then split with SPACE at a specific position to get 50/50

I'm stuck. Let me just accept that the level failed and move on. The next level might have different mechanics that help me understand the solution.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game is over - timer ran out. Let me check if the image shows a horizontal split
# that the segmentation doesn't capture, then try to trigger reset.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
block_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] in [13, 14]]
print("Block nodes:")
for n in block_nodes:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check all nodes near block area (rows 30-45, cols 25-40)
nearby = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if 30 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 45]
print(f"\nNodes near block area: {[(n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels']) for n in nearby]}")

# The image shows purple-on-top white-on-bottom horizontal split
# But segmentation shows solid white (100px) - possible game over display artifact
# Try to trigger level reset by executing an action
result = action(['UP'])
print(f"\nAction result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"SPACE": "At [21,24]: white→merge solid white(100px), purple→swap colors. At [32,17]: white→85/15, purple→65/35 diagonal splits.", "MOUSE on indicator": "Paints id=12 color", "ARROW keys": "UP→[21,24], DOWN→[32,17]"}, "object_taxonomy": {"id_5_9": "Target: white(50) above purple(50), horizontal split", "id_11": "Gray frame, moves UP/DOWN", "id_12": "Diamond in frame, paintable", "id_13": "Block (100px total)"}, "hud_map": {"top_bar": "Color indicators", "bottom_bar": "Timer, depletes each action"}, "win_pattern": "Match left panel: horizontal white-on-top purple-on-bottom 50/50 split", "level_log": {"level_1": "FAILED at 101 actions - timer depleted. Image at game over shows horizontal split (purple-top/white-bottom) - reversed colors. Horizontal split mechanic discovered but colors wrong."}, "cross_level_notes": "Timer ~100 actions. Horizontal split appears at game over - may be the key mechanic."}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Game over state. Block may have horizontal split (image) but segmentation shows solid white. Need to reproduce horizontal split with correct colors before timer expires.", "goal_model": "Horizontal 50/50 white-on-top purple-on-bottom split", "recent_findings": ["Game over image shows horizontal split with reversed colors (purple-top/white-bottom)", "Need to find how to produce horizontal split with correct colors"], "open_questions": ["Does game over trigger horizontal split? Can I reproduce it manually?", "Is there a SPACE sequence that produces horizontal split?"], "current_plan": "Try SPACE sequence: merge→split→SPACE at different position to see if horizontal split emerges", "strategy_log": ["101 actions of SPACE toggling: only diagonal splits", "Game over reveals horizontal split - colors reversed"], "failed_probes": ["All SPACE combos at both positions", "MOUSE on block cells", "Repeated SPACE same color"]}}
</parameter>

nearby = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if 30 <= n['boundary'][0][0] <= 45]
print(f"\nNodes near block area: {[(n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels']) for n in nearby]}")
